pattern: For each pair of darts that are within diameter distance, compute the one or two circle centers of radius r passing through both, then count how many darts lie within radius r of each candidate center.
hints: The optimal circle boundary passes through at least two darts (or has all darts inside it if n=1). Iterate over all pairs (i,j) and find the (up to two) centers of a circle of radius r passing through both points i and j.
For each candidate center, count all darts whose distance from the center is <= r (with a small floating-point epsilon).
The answer is the maximum count found, initialised to 1 (a single dart always fits).

edge_inputs.description: two darts exactly at diameter distance, both fit
four darts on a circle of radius 2
all darts form a tight cluster inside radius
two darts too far apart to share a radius-1 circle
five of six darts fit in the optimal placement
all darts collinear; optimal circle picks middle cluster

stress.description: 20 random darts in a bounded grid
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    seen = set()
    darts = []
    while len(darts) < size:
        x = rng.randint(-50, 50)
        y = rng.randint(-50, 50)
        if (x, y) not in seen:
            seen.add((x, y))
            darts.append([x, y])
    r = rng.randint(5, 30)
    return [darts, r]
